title: Tsurezuregusa: Essays in Idleness
description: A monk's reflections on beauty, impermanence, and the art of living — the Japanese Montaigne.
introduction: A monk's reflections on beauty, impermanence, and the art of living — the Japanese Montaigne.

translation.original_text: つれづれなるままに、日くらし硯にむかひて。
translation.text: In the tedium of idle hours, I sit all day facing my inkstone.

translation.original_text: あだし野の露消ゆる時なく、鳥部山の煙立ち去らでのみ住み果つる習ひならば、いかにもののあはれもなからん。
translation.text: If the dew on the moor of Adashino never vanished, if the smoke on Mount Toribe never cleared, and man lingered on forever — how little the beauty of things would move us.
